calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion For Issuance Of Peremptory Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Mar-10-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER MAX ZARZANA'S MOTION FOR ISSUANCE OF PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDATE. Appearances: Daniel Berko (771.6174) for petitioner and moving party; Nils Rosenquest of Resenquest & Associates (292.0980) for Real Party in Interest Property Management LLC; Francesca Gessner of the Office of the City Attorney (554.4762) for respondent and opposing party. Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied. Petitioner has failed to meet his burden under the substantial evidence test. Review of the administrative records show that the ALJ had substantial evidence before it to conclude that petitioner had a special relationship with the owners and moved into the apartment because of that relationship. Second, there is no record that the petitioner raised the argument of illegally permitted banked increases below. Accordingly, it is not properly before this court. Even if it were, a reading of section 6.11(a)(2) does not support petitioner's argument. Third, there was sufficient evidence to support the rental rate determinations. Finally, petitioner was not entitled to an appeal of the ALJ's recalculation of rent on remand. Order signed in open court. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
